title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Stephen Megalos informs the group that Tom wrote a note to the police as a final warning before Megalos could return to Bradwood. Amidst the rising tension, Ellery Queen argues that the killer, Croscius (also referred to as Croscott, Crostach, or Crocker), is acting alone. Ellery bases this deduction on the extreme secrecy surrounding the Tewell and Crosby family feud, Croscius's lack of funds to hire a co-conspirator, and the recurring "T" left at crime scenes. Ellery also suggests that Croscius's limp might be a pretense; while the Tewell brothers recall Crostach having a limp as a child due to hip disease, it is unknown if the condition ever healed.

When the police officer Vorn proposes providing heavy protection for the brothers, Ellery warns that a large police presence would alert Croscius to the location of Andrew Tewell. Ellery advises that Andrew, who is currently disguised as "Old Pete," should be discreetly escorted back to his mountain cabin in Arroyo to avoid drawing attention. 

Andrew expresses a desire to renounce his inheritance from his brother Tomislav and flee to escape the "devil," but he eventually agrees to remain in character. Megalos offers to advance Andrew five thousand dollars, noting that as the eldest brother, Andrew is adhering to strict inheritance customs. Despite the offer, Andrew insists on using his own savings. Megalos eventually sits at a desk to write a check for the money while Andrew G. Tewell prepares to depart.
